data.body:
PASS 1 review — [T3] Integration feasibility (overlay). VERDICT: PASS with one minor coaching item (the optional OpenAPI leg).

AFFIRMATIONS (T3 sub-checks satisfied, tool-grounded):
- (a) technical_feasibility + (b) named-method/endpoint existence — MATCH. The dual-run shape-contract harness targets a surface that already exists in production: get_comment_map (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_graph.py:289), get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py:391), get_parent_map (acli_graph.py:189), and search_issues. The underlying endpoint POST /rest/api/3/search/jql is live in production (acli_graph.py:236/337/431 via _direct_rest_post_json). This is an INTERNAL, already-used integration — not an asserted-but-unverified external capability.
- (c) auth/HTTPS preconditions STATED + grounded. HTTPS Basic auth over the configured jira_url with env-only api_token (acli_rest.py:103/128; fetcher.py:298 constructs AcliClient(jira_url, user, api_token)). The live leg's gate is real and proven: REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1 + the external marker + per-test credential skipif (tests/external/conftest.py:37). AC1/AC4 ("live-gated marker", "opt-in, no flake/slowdown") correctly reuse this existing seam.

ONE COACHING ITEM (the only externally-new capability the plan asserts):
- [ ] Route the optional OpenAPI-validation work to an explicit SPIKE (investigate-before-commit), or keep it explicitly gated, before relying on it — confirm Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI actually models the nested comment/issuelinks/parent shapes you intend to validate.
  - criteria: [T3]
  - location: story 59ef §What bullet 4 ("Optional: validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI where the spec covers the field — investigate spec completeness first — see epic open question").
  - evidence: This is the one capability NOT already used in the repo — a repo search for "OpenAPI" returns no client-side validator. Per T3(d) a capability gap should route to a SPIKE. The plan ALREADY does the right thing (marks it "Optional", caveats "investigate spec completeness first", defers to an epic open question), and the latest ticket comment resolves it as out-of-scope/YAGNI for a single team — so this is a confirm-and-keep-deferred nudge, not a blocker.
  - suggested_fix: (left empty — the outcome depends on the spike's finding about spec coverage, which I can't determine from the plan text; dropping the leg, as the latest remediation note proposes, also satisfies T3.)
